description: pip tar/ZIP concatenated file recognition behavior conflict, may install unexpected content
reason: upstream advisory currently has no fixed version published
mitigation: upgrade pip to >=26.1 for CVE-2026-6357
install only from trusted indexes
avoid untrusted sdists/wheels
keep audit failure active for all other CVEs
review_trigger: remove ignore once fixed version appears
resolution_criteria: CVE-2026-3219 fixed version published on PyPI advisory DB

description: product-tests job fails in CI on ubuntu-latest with no annotations, while all 737 tests pass locally in every configuration tested (clean env, PYTHONDEVMODE=1, -W error reveals 16 warnings but no errors).
reason: Likely CI runner environment difference — cannot reproduce locally across 5+ configurations
mitigation: All 737 tests pass locally (uv run pytest tests/unit/product -q)
governance-self-check, verify-native, ruff, governance-tests all pass in CI
This is a CI signal quality issue, not a code defect
Re-run CI job to check if transient
If persistent: add verbose output (remove -q) to CI step for diagnosis
review_trigger: Re-run CI product-tests job or push new commit
resolution_criteria: product-tests passes in CI or root cause identified with evidence
resolution: Fixed: replaced hardcoded /root/projects/Ordivon with dynamic Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] in test_ordivon_verify_registry_reconciler_regression.py. CI path differs from local path. Commit bd98354.

description: Debt JSONL ledger has no entry-level integrity protection. Debt status can be changed from OPEN to CLOSED with no evidence, and ordivon-verify does NOT detect the change. The verification-debt checker validates ledger structure but not individual entry integrity.
reason: JSONL ledgers are plain text — no checksum, audit trail, or signature on individual entries
mitigation: Short-term: add debt-entry integrity check to verification-debt checker
Medium-term: add content-hash to each debt entry
Long-term: consider signed commits or git-based audit trail
review_trigger: Next red-team audit or debt system modification
resolution_criteria: Modifying debt entry status without evidence is detected and BLOCKED by at least one CI checker
resolution: RT-09 fixed (Wave 2): content hashes added to dependency-audit-debts.jsonl and lesson-ledger.jsonl via scripts/hash_ledger.py. CI governance-self-check verifies hash integrity on every push. Tampering with debt status without updating hash → BLOCKED. Commit: pending.

description: Checker Python source code can be modified to inject blind spots (e.g., adding entries to GOVERNED_EXCLUSIONS). No CI job verifies checker source integrity or detects modification of governance-critical constants. An unregistered file was successfully hidden by modifying the exclusion list.
reason: Checker source code has no integrity verification — modifications are git-tracked but not automatically detected post-merge
mitigation: Short-term: add checker integrity self-check to governance-self-check CI job
Medium-term: extract all authority sets (exclusions, safe files, etc.) to governed JSON schemas
Long-term: content-addressable checker manifests with hash verification
review_trigger: Next checker source modification or red-team audit
resolution_criteria: Checker source modification that creates a governance blind spot is detected by CI
resolution: RT-11 fixed (Wave 1): GOVERNED_EXCLUSIONS extracted to docs/governance/schemas/governed-exclusions.json. Checker now loads exclusions from schema (L-CI-SELFCAL-002 pattern). CI governance-self-check verifies schema integrity. Commit: pending.

description: 5 review_learning lessons from DG-Checkerization (L-DG-CHECKERIZATION-003/004/005) and CI-SelfCalibration (L-CI-SELFCAL-005) need human review and classification. Review script deployed (review_lessons.py). Not automated — requires human decision.
reason: review_learning lessons require domain judgment, not automatable
mitigation: Run review_lessons.py to see pending lessons
Classify each as CandidateRule, methodology update, or close

description: 3 checkers are draft: checker-maturity (self-referential), lesson-extraction (pipeline incomplete), policy-shadow (advisory). checker-maturity being draft means no automated checker maturity enforcement.
reason: Low priority — draft checkers are all advisory/secondary. Hardening requires dedicated phase.
mitigation: Harden checker-maturity when governance system is stable
Connect lesson-extraction to review_lessons.py pipeline

description: 233 files on disk are not in governed directories. Most are intentionally excluded: legacy archives, runtime receipts, audit reports, ADRs, design docs, runbooks, plans. No urgent gap — scope expansion assessed as low value.
reason: Files are either intentionally excluded or governed by other mechanisms (design-pack-contract, ADR self-documentation)
mitigation: Periodic review of scope boundaries
Register if governance need emerges

description: 495 source/test/frontend/database files are not in document registry. Code is governed by architecture-boundaries checker and language tooling, not by document governance. Acknowledged as intentional boundary — these files do not need registry entries.
review_trigger: When PM coverage boundary is next reviewed or new file types enter governed scope
removal_condition: Either classified as governed (if governance scope expands), generated (if auto-derived), fixture (if test), or kept as intentional A4 debt
